title: Bodies of 15 Indians killed in Vietnam boat tragedy arrive in Mumbai
content: The bodies of fifteen Indian nationals killed in Vietnam's boat tragedy have arrived in Mumbai. Ten victims were from Tamil Nadu, three from Andhra Pradesh, and two from Kerala. One Indian national remains hospitalized in Phu Quoc after a medical procedure. The speedboat carrying thirty-two Indian tourists capsized near Phu Quoc Island on Friday. Vietnamese authorities have ordered an investigation into the incident and detained the operator.

title: 'No French players in France ': Ex-Spain PM's remark sparks racism row
content: Former Spanish Prime Minister Mariano Rajoy sparked outrage after saying France's football team had "no French players" ahead of the World Cup semi-final. Leaders in Spain and France condemned the remarks as xenophobic and racist. France defended the diversity of its national team, stressing all players are French citizens and should be judged by talent, not their background.

title: Where are the Gandhi siblings? Why Wayanad has put Rahul, Priyanka in line of BJP fire
content: The BJP is raising eyebrows over Rahul and Priyanka Gandhi's absence from Wayanad following a landslide, labeling them as part-time politicians and political tourists. Wayanad is crucial for the Gandhis, as it has been a significant political stronghold for Rahul. Meanwhile, Rahul has not been seen publicly for almost three weeks. In response, Congress defends their leaders, stating their absence helps maintain the focus on relief operations.

title: How Apple may have ensured that it is end of OpenAI's 'smartphone dream'
content: Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI is significantly hindering its secretive hardware project.  The litigation threatens OpenAI's engineering pipeline and its ability to recruit talent.  OpenAI had aimed to launch its first consumer hardware product later this year.  However, legal scrutiny now makes it difficult for the company to proceed.  This legal action effectively derails OpenAI's hardware ambitions and future plans.

title: 'Threat to US sovereignty': Why Trump wants to 'dismantle' International Criminal Court
content: The Trump administration initiated a campaign against the International Criminal Court. This action targets the court's authority over American officials and military personnel.  The United States argues the court threatens national sovereignty and self-governance. Diplomatic efforts and potential sanctions will be employed to disable the court. This move aims to protect American citizens from international prosecution.

title: Big changes in Chess World Cup! FIDE approves new format for 2027
content: FIDE has approved a new format for the 2027 World Cup and Women's World Cup, reducing the events from 27 to 19 days by introducing a Swiss qualification stage before the knockout rounds. Both tournaments will feature more players, expanded qualification pathways and a combined prize fund of USD 3.3 million, making the competitions more inclusive and rewarding.

title: What parents can learn from Kylian Mbappé's journey from a football-mad kid to a global superstar
content: Kylian Mbappé's journey from a young fan to a football star offers parenting insights.  Parents should recognize and support children's interests and passions early on.  Discipline and dedication are crucial alongside natural talent for sustained success.  Role models can inspire positive development without creating undue pressure.  Believing in children's dreams fosters confidence and encourages their best efforts.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
content: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the nonprofit's history. Active Minds focuses on youth mental health awareness and reducing stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support young leaders. This donation occurs amid growing concerns about youth mental health.

title: Britain bought lifeless farmland for £13.8M, now beavers help it earn
content: A farm in Lincolnshire is evolving into a lucrative wildland endeavor, with beavers playing a pivotal role in this ecological project. The initiative focuses on demonstrating that restoring nature can lead to substantial private income. Current revenue streams are generated through carbon removal and biodiversity credits, with aspirations to draw significant investments for sustainable environmental solutions.

title: From teen sweethearts to soulmates: Patrick Swayze and Lisa Niemi
content: Patrick Swayze and Lisa Niemi's love story remains one of Hollywood's most enduring romances. Even years after the Road House star's death in 2009 following a battle with pancreatic cancer, Lisa still becomes emotional when she remembers the first time they danced together. What started as dance rehearsals during their teenage years soon blossomed into a romance that lasted more than three decades.

title: Satya Nadella warns AI users: You are paying for your own IP, suggests 5 solutions
content: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella says enterprises using AI are paying twice—once in cash, once in proprietary knowledge. In a post viewed 5.7 million times, he calls it the "Reverse Information Paradox": every prompt, eval and correction leaks institutional know-how to model providers. He flags the irony of fair use for vendors, restrictive terms for buyers, and offers a five-point fix: Control, Capability, Choice, Cost, Compound.

title: Trump announces 20% toll on ships in Strait of Hormuz
content: Trump's announcement comes after another major exchange of fire cast fresh uncertainty over efforts to negotiate an end to the conflict. Before Iran asserted control over the Strait of Hormuz after the war began, about one-fifth of the world's oil and gas supplies moved through the strategic waterway.

title: 'We will test them out': Duckett issues warning to Rohit, Virat ahead of ODI series
content: England opener Ben Duckett stated they will test Indian batters with short balls.  He acknowledged the challenge will differ from the T20 series.  Duckett also discussed facing Jasprit Bumrah in the ODI format.  He believes England players have individual plans for Bumrah.  Despite T20 success, England anticipates a tough ODI series challenge.

title: Canucks explore trade options for former superstar Elias Pettersson
content: Elias Pettersson surrounded by NHL trade rumors as Vancouver Canucks look ahead to the future. His inconsistent play has made his trade value, as the former franchise centerpiece, suspect throughout the league. Vancouver will now consider potential offers, contract issues and whether trading Pettersson could accelerate a roster shake-up this offseason.

title: Sid Seixeiro questions Auston Matthews' leadership after Marner comments
content: Craig Berube unveiled Mitch Marner’s significant emotional presence in the locker room, and Sid Seixeiro has started a conversation on the leadership make-up of the Toronto Maple Leafs. The comments have raised questions about Auston Matthews’ captaincy, team culture and the impact of losing Marner. The discussion comes after a disappointing 2025-26 season in Toronto without the longtime forward.

title: Jude Bellingham credits his parents for shaping his football journey
content: Denise and Mark Bellingham have been central to Jude Bellingham’s rise from a young footballer in Birmingham to one of England’s biggest stars. Through career sacrifices, constant support, and important decisions, they have helped shape both Jude and Jobe’s careers. While reports have questioned their relationship, they continue to stand behind their sons’ success.

title: FIFA WC semis cheatsheet: Can England stop Messi, will Spain vanquish France?
content: France, Argentina, Spain and England are the last four standing. Here’s the not-so-brief guide to the World Cup’s first semi-final line-up featuring the top four ranked teams.

title: How Israel's Mossad tried to recruit Iran's former president Ahmadinejad
content: Israel's Mossad reportedly sought to recruit Mahmoud Ahmadinejad for regime change. Secret meetings and financial aid were allegedly provided to the former Iranian president. An attempted extraction from Tehran failed after Ahmadinejad grew dissatisfied with the plan. Iranian authorities later placed Ahmadinejad under house arrest after uncovering his alleged contacts. No official confirmations have been issued by either involved nation.

title: Ahead of England ODIs, captain Gill reveals 2027 WC plans for Kohli, Rohit
content: India captain Shubman Gill reaffirmed that Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma remain central to the ODI team's 2027 World Cup plans, calling them the “backbone” of India’s batting. Emphasising their experience in pressure situations, Gill said India’s settled core will stay intact while younger players are gradually integrated through opportunities in upcoming ODI series.

title: Why Dubai's housing market slowed in 2026; sales fell 16%, prices dipped by 7%
content: Dubai's residential property sales saw a sixteen percent decrease in early 2026. This slowdown occurred as regional geopolitical tensions impacted buyer sentiment and demand. Despite this, sales remained fifteen percent higher than in the first half of 2024. Residential property prices saw a slight decline between February and April. However, 2025 was a record year for Dubai's property market.

title: Meet Ailén Cova, the woman by Alexis Mac Allister's side
content: Liverpool and the Argentina midfielder Alexis Mac Allister has enjoyed huge success on the football field. Away from football, his personal life has also remained in the spotlight. His relationship with Argentine fashion designer and influencer Ailen Cova has attracted attention. The couple made their love public in late 2022.

title: 'I would be very surprised': Karthik on Andy Flower becoming England Test coach
content: Dinesh Karthik believes Andy Flower would be an excellent choice for England's Test coach.  However, he feels Flower's existing commitments make this move highly unlikely.  Flower has a three-year contract with Royal Challengers Bengaluru for the IPL.  This would mean missing parts of England's Test matches.  The schedule presents significant challenges for such a dual role.

title: Pakistan faces shortage of 100 life-saving medicines like cancer drugs, morphine
content: Pakistan faces a critical shortage of over 100 essential medicines, including life-saving drugs.  Delayed price revisions by the Drug Regulatory Authority of Pakistan are disrupting production.  Rising costs of raw materials and currency depreciation have impacted manufacturing expenses significantly.  This situation is creating space for counterfeit and substandard products in the market.  Industry representatives urge the government to approve pending hardship pricing cases immediately.

title: Who guards Hormuz and who gets paid? Trump and Iran clash over control of the strait
content: The conflict between Iran and the United States over the Strait of Hormuz continues. Both nations are contesting control of the vital waterway amid ongoing attacks. The United States conducted significant strikes on Iranian targets following an attack on a container ship. Iran stated it alone has authority to control the Strait of Hormuz. Commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has increasingly shifted out of public view.

title: Watch: Iran fires missile towards US targets with 'GAME OVER USA' painted on it
content: Iran launched a ballistic missile with "GAME OVER USA" painted on its body. This action followed recent United States strikes on Iranian military targets. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed missile and drone operations against American facilities. These strikes targeted bases in Jordan, Bahrain, and Kuwait. Iran warned against further foreign military interference in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Telangana POCSO accused, wanted for slaughtering 6, found dead after 60-hour manhunt
content: In a tragic turn of events, an accused in a POCSO case was discovered deceased following a shocking series of murders. He reportedly took the lives of six individuals, including three innocent children and members of his own family. This individual had a disturbing history, having previously murdered the mother and grandmother of his victim. After abducting a teenager, he ended her life near a lakeside.

title: A US inmate demanded pasta in Tihar: What prison meal looks like in different countries
content: A US citizen arrested in a terror case seeks court permission to cook his own food. He has been on a hunger strike for over 50 days due to prison food. Matthew Aaron VanDyke claims jail meals are unsuitable for his dietary habits. His family is willing to cover all expenses for his personal cooking setup. The court has sought a response from Tihar Jail authorities.

title: Youth thrashed by mob while with woman of another faith in UP
content: A youth was allegedly assaulted by a mob in Muzaffarnagar after being found with a woman of a different faith. A video of the incident surfaced online, prompting an immediate police investigation into the matter. Police reached the spot and rescued both individuals from the agitated crowd. Officials are currently verifying identities and investigating the circumstances surrounding the confrontation.

title: IND vs ENG 1st ODI: Joe Root returns as England announce playing XI - See full team
content: Jacob Bethell will open batting with Ben Duckett for England's first ODI. Jos Buttler plays his 200th ODI match, marking a significant career milestone. England's bowling attack features a spin-heavy approach against India's strong batting lineup. Jofra Archer returns to the ODI squad after a strong T20I performance. The three-match series begins at Edgbaston on Tuesday.

title: 'Get ready for sudden death': Iran media's Lindsey reference in warning to Trump, Bibi
content: Iranian newspapers featured Senator Lindsey Graham's death on their front pages. Some publications issued veiled warnings directed at the US and its leaders. One newspaper described Graham as the "Merchant of Death" following his sudden illness. State media celebrated his passing, calling him "sent to hell." These remarks fueled speculation among some supporters of President Trump's movement.

title: Anupam Kher reacts to 6-year old video of Naseeruddin, responds to trolling over Ram Mandir comments
content: Anupam Kher addressed an old video of Naseeruddin Shah calling him a clown. He stated that he and Naseeruddin Shah respect each other and have met recently. Kher emphasized his achievements are due to hard work and divine grace. He believes trolls are trying to target him after his Ram Janmbhoomi comments. Kher concluded by asserting that self-made individuals are not afraid of abuse.

title: 'Why the axe is always on Sanju?': Ex-chief selector blasts Gambhir-led management
content: MSK Prasad advised patience regarding coach Gautam Gambhir's early tenure.  He noted key players Hardik Pandya and Jasprit Bumrah were absent during recent losses.  Prasad stated assessing Gambhir requires a full-strength team for future matches.  He also criticized the decision to drop Sanju Samson after a few low scores.  The former selector also expressed concern over Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's gradual introduction.

title: Days after love marriage, Bengaluru woman dies by suicide after husband 'delays' visit
content: A tragic incident near Bengaluru has left a newly-married woman dead after she ingested poison. Authorities believe she may have taken her own life following a conversation with her husband, Ravindra, who had eloped with her after a whirlwind romance. His inability to be with her right away seems to have caused her emotional turmoil. Investigations are underway to uncover the full details surrounding her demise.

title: India's 40-year-old streak in England continues with historic Lord's win
content: India’s historic 270-run win over England in the first women’s Test at Lord’s extended their unbeaten Test record in England to 40 years. Yastika Bhatia’s landmark century and Kranti Gaud’s seven-wicket match haul powered the victory. India now lead the overall head-to-head 4-1, with 11 draws, underlining their growing dominance in women’s Test cricket.

title: Watch: Iran 'completely destroys' Patriot air defences, fuel tanks, radar at US Gulf bases
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guard claimed missile and drone strikes on US bases in three countries. These operations targeted air defense systems and fuel depots in retaliation. The IRGC stated the attacks were a response to recent American military strikes. US Central Command confirmed precision strikes against Iranian military installations. Both sides engaged in escalating military actions in the region.

title: ED receives copies of documents in alleged ‘horse-trading’ case
content: The agency is likely to probe the money laundering angle in the case. The police had seized ₹60 lakh of unaccounted cash from one of the suspects

title: Panel headed by Sudha Pillai to study Kerala Infrastructure Investment Fund Board revamp
content: The tenure of the five-member committee is three months from the date of the July 13 order. The panel will also examine the resource mobilisation strategy adopted by KIIFB

title: Why Tamil Nadu does not need privatisation of power distribution
content: Government funding narrows the gap between average cost of supply and average revenue realisation of the TNPDCL, says the White Paper

title: Assam Rifles jawan killed in Nagaland IED blast
content: The incident, which injured four others, occurred when a convoy of vehicles was returning from Sukhovi to Dimapur town

title: Nine bodies recovered from trawler that went missing off Bengal coast; six fishermen missing
content: The  , Joy Maa Kaali, set sail from Purba Medinipur; it went missing on July 2 and was found in the Sundarbans; the crew gave a distress call on July 8 about engine failure and sought help, says fishermen’s union

title: Saffron paint of Academy of Fine Arts security room sparks protest; theatre artists write to BJP Bengal chief
content: Since the BJP came to power in West Bengal in May, several government buildings, streets, and civic structures have been witnessing a colour change, including some sections of State secretariat Nabanna

title: Kuki-Zo Council meets IB chief, seeks govt. action on blockade by Nagas
content: Kuki-Zo Council members say restoration of the popular government in Manipur on February 4, a year after President’s Rule was imposed, has been of “no good” to the people

title: Supreme Court refuses to hear Telangana’s plea against Polavaram project
content: The top court said that the suit could not be taken up for hearing until the Telangana government complied with the requisite procedural formalities

title: Flight carrying bodies of 15 Indians killed in Vietnam boat tragedy lands at Mumbai airport
content: Of the 15 victims, 10 were from Tamil Nadu, three were from Andhra Pradesh, and two were from Kerala. Two of the deceased were women.

title: India-Australia ‘administrative arrangements’ for uranium supplies | Explained
content: The recent agreement paves the way for Australian uranium exports to India, allowing private sector collaboration under stringent IAEA oversight

title: BJP will lay siege to KRS reservoir if water is not released for standing crops, warns Leader of Opposition in Legislative Assembly R. Ashok
content: The government must immediately release water from the KRS reservoir and hold an all-party meeting to discuss the issue of sharing Cauvery water with Tamil Nadu, he said.

title: Supreme Court questions misuse of POCSO in teen relationships
content: Bench observes that parents often resort to criminal proceedings to protect their so-called ‘honour’ when teenage girls elope with their partners

title: Autonomous hill councils to be extended to all seven districts in Ladakh
content: The Ladakh administration has added 17 additional tehsils taking the total to 32; Chief Secretary Ashish Kundra says several rounds of discussions have been held with representatives of religious organisations and civil society bodies over constitutional safeguards

title: Tamil Nadu Government transfers DVAC chief A. Arun, appoints IG Mageshwari
content: A. Arun was appointed as the Director of the DVAC on May 25 and has now been transferred within two months of taking charge

title: IAS officers transferred in Tamil Nadu
content: TNMSC MD H. Krishnanunni posted as Director of Animal Husbandry and Veterinary Services; S.P. Amrith replaces Mr. Krishnaunni; Labour Commissioner Shilpa Prabhakar Satish replaces P. Umanath as MD of Tamil Nadu Civil Supplies Corporation

title: Mysuru: Free women’s oncology clinic, HPV vaccination drive to be organised on July 15
content: The clinic and vaccination drive will be held at the BHIO Day Care Clinic, Kuvempunagar, from 1 p.m. to 4 p.m.

title: Judicial custody of all eight accused in Ram Temple donation embezzlement case extended
content: Court of Additional District Judge Pratibha Narayan to decide the prosecution’s request for police remand of two of the eight accused on Tuesday

title: No shortage of fertilisers in NTR district, says Collector
content: Lakshmisha says the government follows a transparent fertiliser distribution system through the A.P. Agribusiness Information Management System app, and fertilisers are given to farmers based on online land records

title: Agriculture officers urge govt. to roll back farmer-officer connect scheme 2.0
content: The officers say this scheme would be detrimental to the interests of farmers as agricultural officers who focus on staple crops cannot give advice on horticulture crops

title: Software testing firm TestingXperts opens 300-seater delivery centre in Hyderabad
content: 200 people onboarded; number likely to be 500 within a year; long-term plan is for a 2,500-seat campus in Hyderabad, TestingXperts leadership says

title: Dhruva Space secures ₹60-crore investment from space-tech fund AVCF
content: 'It is the first company to receive funding from the dedicated space-tech fund anchored by IN-SPACe and managed by SIDBI Venture Capital'

title: Tahir Hussain, four others convicted in IB staff Ankit Sharma murder case
content: Former councillor is convicted for murder, kidnapping with intent to confine, rioting with deadly weapons, among other serious charges, and acquitted on charges of hatching a criminal conspiracy and abetment of mutiny

title: TANKER Foundation distributes CMCHIS-supported CAPD fluid bags
content: President and founder-trustee of TANKER Foundation Georgi Abraham said that introducing CAPD in India in 1991 was the beginning of a journey to make home-based dialysis a reality for patients

title: At Jantar Mantar, fasting student hospitalised while Wangchuk loses 8 kg in CJP hunger strike
content: Sonam Wangchuk, who has been fasting on the dais at Jantar Mantar’s protest site, has lost over 8 kilograms, the Cockroach Janta Party said as strike crosses Day 16

title: Supreme Court rejects claims of over 350 teachers of West Bengal madrasas for state salary
content: The case came up before the apex court with questions, including whether the madrasas in which the petitioners were appointed were validly recognised

title: Border States flag illegal migrants on voter rolls, use of drones, apps by smugglers
content: SPs of 119 border districts attend meeting; lack of infrastructure and opportunities in border villages discussed; increased use of drones along Pakistan border to smuggle weapons and drugs highlighted

title: Alampur Montessori School honours founder with ‘Sphoorthi 2026’ celebrations
content: Teachers and students pay homage by presenting speeches, poems, and songs highlighting various aspects of Kalyanamma’s inspiring life and her remarkable contribution to education

title: Chief Minister Vijay inaugurates MLA office in his Perambur constituency
content: He also inaugurated the e-Sevai (e-Service) centre on the premises, held discussions with officials on the integrated development projects for Perambur, and received petitions from members of the public

title: Development works worth ₹500 crore underway in Kondepi, says Minister
content: Inaugurating a road project at Singarayakonda, Dola Sree Bala Veeranjaneya Swamy criticises the previous government for halting BC residential school project in Singarayakonda, adding that ₹45 crore has been sanctioned to it by the BC Welfare Minister

title: SC allows CBI, ED to lead Reliance ADAG Group probe, says it cannot decide the course of investigation
content: Any comment made by the Supreme Court during an ongoing investigation, even though unintentional, would impact the trial, Chief Justice Surya Kant says; court allows investigative agencies to file their status reports

title: YSRCP announces Statewide protest for Rayalaseema Lift Irrigation Project completion
content: Alliance government stalled the the project despite payment of over ₹900 crore in bills and 80% completion of works during YSRCP regime, claims party’s State coordinator

title: Registration of car driven by Sub-Inspector’s six-year-old granddaughter cancelled for one year
content: The registrations of more than 4,050 vehicles found to have been driven by minors were cancelled across Telangana during the last financial year

title: Naidu reviews Polavaram works, stresses timely completion
content: Chief Minister directs officials to complete remaining ECRF dam works, expedite rehabilitation and infrastructure, and reviews progress of 36 priority irrigation projects across Andhra Pradesh

title: Residents flag dangerous road conditions across East Bengaluru’s IT corridor
content: These roads serve major residential areas, IT corridor, schools and hospitals but are filled with potholes and pose a risk to motorists

title: BRS Launches Fresh Campaign Against Congress In Telangana Over Unfulfilled Job Promises
content: As part of this effort to put pressure on the ruling Congress, the party will hold a public meeting called "Yuva Sangrama Sadassu" on July 18 at Saroornagar Stadium in Hyderabad.

title: Nashik Family Chased For 15 km, Car Smashed After Resisting Molestation
content: They attempted to beat the family members with wooden sticks and iron rods and severely vandalised their vehicle.

title: Guwahati Submerged After Heavy Rain, Thousands Left Stranded In Traffic Jams
content: One carriageway of the highway was submerged after floodwater overflowed onto the road, forcing authorities to divert traffic.

title: 1 Killed In Maine Shooting Involving ICE, State House Speaker Says
content: Immigration officials said in late January that they had ceased "enhanced operations" in Maine after making hundreds of arrests.

title: Ex-AAP Leader Tahir Hussain Convicted For Intelligence Officer's Murder During Delhi Riots
content: The Karkardooma Court found Tahir Hussain, who was suspended by the AAP after his name came up in the case, guilty under the charges of promoting enmity, rioting, assault, criminal force and murder.

title: Video: US Strikes Iranian Submarine Centre Using Sea Drones For First Time
content: Three Corsair unmanned surface vessels carried out the strike at the crucial Bandar Abbas port. This was the first time American forces have employed sea drones in combat operations.

title: Hash Oil Valued In Crores Seized, Peddlers Among 7 Arrested In Hyderabad
content: Of them, three peddlers were arrested in Jubilee Hills after the interception of a drug distribution network.

title: Russian 'Doomsday' Plane Lands In Iran Amid Conflict, Sparks Curiosity
content: Often referred to in the news media as the "Doomsday Plane", the Tu-214PU serves primarily as a specialised, hardened communications node rather than Russia's primary nuclear-survivable airborne command platform.

title: Decades Of Silence, Then $407 Million Answer: Japan Is Building A Spy Agency
content: At the heart of it all is Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, who sees a new domestic agency as central to her broader ambition of freeing Japan from the security constraints it has carried since the Second World War.

title: Stabbed 51 Times, Body Dumped In Drain: A Murder That Shook Riot-Hit Delhi
content: In February 2020, northeast Delhi plunged into turmoil over the protests for and against the Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA) and the National Register of Citizens (NRC) exercise

title: The Honesty Deficit: How Petty Theft Is Holding India Back
content: Many people consider it a practical observation about human behaviour. But if honesty exists only when there is no opportunity to be dishonest, then what does that say about us as a society?

title: US To Charge 20% On Cargo Shipped Through Strait Of Hormuz: Trump
content: The US president asserted that the strait is open and added that the US is reinstating the blockade to stop Iranian ships from entering or leaving the country.

title: US Senator Lindsey Graham's Sudden Death Fuel 'Assassination' Speculation
content: The Republican veteran, one of US President Donald Trump's closest allies and a leading voice on US foreign policy, died on Saturday.

title: Omar Abdullah's "Hiding Behind Court" Jibe At BJP Over Rs 100 Crore Notice
content: The notice served by the BJP seeks an apology from Omar Abdullah or evidence to corroborate his allegation that the BJP offered Rs 20 to Rs 30 crore to National Conference legislators to switch sides.

title: Man Behind Bangladesh's Tallest Ram Statue Arrested On Fraud Charges
content: Das, who had proposed building an 81-foot statue of Lord Ram at the Sri Sri Radha Gobinda Kali Temple in Palashbari in Gaibandha, has been arrested in a money laundering case involving suspicious financial transactions worth Tk9.35 crore.

title: Rs 1,160 Crore Rice 'Scam' Widens: 56 Mills, 22 Ethanol Plants Under Scanner
content: Investigators are trying to establish how much of the nearly 5 lakh metric tonnes of government rice allocated for ethanol production was actually converted into ethanol, and how much may have been diverted through private mills before returning to government warehouses.

title: Trump's Iran War Closed Hormuz Strait. Now He Wants 'Fee' For Guarding It
content: Trump said the US had effectively been guarding the strait "for nothing" up to now, but that this would change, with wealthy nations expected to pay for the arrangement going forward.

title: PM Modi To Visit Punjab, Launch Rail Projects As BJP Gears Up For Solo Fight
content: PM Modi will inaugurate the redeveloped Jalandhar Cantonment Railway Station, a 110-year-old station refurbished by the Central government as per the Amrit Bharat Station Scheme.

title: Beyond the 70% headline: H-1B workers becoming more educated, better paid
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More
